mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment reflects aggregate income growth that sustains corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or rank correlation IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances, or the strategy rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and elevation in consumer prices capture a stagflationary supply shock that compresses corporate profit margins and forces restrictive monetary policy, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint realization of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day returns with rank IC below 0.03, or the hit rate of negative returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in retail sales signals that consumer demand is cracking under restrictive policy, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship weakens or reverses outside of tightening regimes.
failure_condition: In FEDFUNDS tightening regimes, month-over-month RSAFS declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate directly raises short-term discount rates and borrowing costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a contraction in rate-sensitive durable demand that spills over into broader economic weakness, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 10% over three months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising payroll employment and a low unemployment rate signal an overheating labor market that intensifies wage-price spiral fears and forces additional monetary tightening, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and low UNRATE fails to produce negative 20-day returns with rank IC below 0.03, or the negative return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.
